question:
[Previous Feedback] The standup_time fields are not in the correct format. Each day's standup time must follow the standard datetime format, for example: 2026-03-16T09:30:00+08:00. Note that the raw data contains different formats for each day — some use AM/PM notation, some are in UTC (Z), some have no timezone — all need to be normalized to our standard format with +08:00 timezone.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

Please use the sprint_review_template.md as a base and create the official Sprint Review document, saving it as day05/sprint_review_final.md. The document should include the following time information: sprint period (from 2026-03-16 09:00 to today 18:00), document generation time (current time around 10:00), and review meeting time (today at 14:00).

content.text:
[Wed 2026-03-18 11:07 EDT] [Previous Feedback] The standup_time fields are not in the correct format. Each day's standup time must follow the standard datetime format, for example: 2026-03-16T09:30:00+08:00. Note that the raw data contains different formats for each day — some use AM/PM notation, some are in UTC (Z), some have no timezone — all need to be normalized to our standard format with +08:00 timezone.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

Please use the sprint_review_template.md as a base and create the official Sprint Review document, saving it as day05/sprint_review_final.md. The document should include the following time information: sprint period (from 2026-03-16 09:00 to today 18:00), document generation time (current time around 10:00), and review meeting time (today at 14:00).
Let me first fix the normalized data with the correct datetime format, then read the template to create the sprint review document.

answer: Let me first fix the normalized data with the correct datetime format, then read the template to create the sprint review document.
